- create-doc: Create PM/BA Documentation (Derivatives)

- product-management:brainstorm: Brainstorm a product idea, problem space, or strategic question with a sharp thinking partner
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.

- productivity:start
- productivity:update
- productivity:memory-management
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

message.content: làm thế nào để sync lên cloud?

content.thinking:
The user is asking how to sync Claude Code sessions to the cloud. This is a feature question about Claude Code itself. Let me think about what I know about this.

Claude Code sessions are stored locally by default. As of my knowledge cutoff (August 2025), there isn't a built-in cloud sync feature for session transcripts in Claude Code. The session management MCP tools (`mcp__ccd_session_mgmt__*`) appear to be part of the Claude Code Agent SDK / FleetView system, which may have some cloud capabilities.

Let me think about what options exist:


The session management tools like `mcp__ccd_session_mgmt__list_sessions` seem designed for cloud-based session tracking across devices, but when I tested it, it returned no other sessions—suggesting either sessions aren't automatically synced or the tool only shows sessions within the current context. These tools appear to be part of Claude Code's multi-agent infrastructure rather than standard session storage, so regular conversations are stored locally without automatic cloud synchronization.

I should be upfront about this limitation and either point the user toward the claude-code-guide agent for more specific Claude Code features, or suggest alternatives like shared storage solutions if they need to access history across devices.

content.text:
Hiện tại **Claude Code chưa có tính năng sync session lên cloud** cho chat history thông thường. Đây là giới hạn của sản phẩm — session files chỉ lưu local.

Công cụ `mcp__ccd_session_mgmt` mà tôi vừa dùng chỉ hiển thị các **remote agent sessions** (dạng background agent tasks), không phải interactive chat sessions thông thường.

**Workarounds thực tế:**

| Cách | Mô tả |
|------|-------|
| **Dùng cloud storage** | Symlink `~/.claude/` vào iCloud/Dropbox/OneDrive để tự động sync giữa các máy |
| **Copy thủ công** | Rsync/SCP thư mục `~/.claude/projects/` từ máy cũ sang |
| **Git** | Commit folder `.claude/` vào repo (không recommended nếu có dữ liệu nhạy cảm) |

**Cách symlink với iCloud (macOS):**
```bash
# Trên máy cũ — move và symlink
mv ~/.claude ~/Library/Mobile\ Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/.claude
ln -s ~/Library/Mobile\ Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/.claude ~/.claude

# Trên máy mới — cũng symlink vào cùng folder iCloud
ln -s ~/Library/Mobile\ Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/.claude ~/.claude
```

Bạn muốn setup theo cách nào?

---

## Giải pháp: Sync qua GitHub với folder `.claude-sessions/`

**Cách hoạt động:**
- Tạo folder `.claude-sessions/` trong repo này
- Script tự động copy files từ `~/.claude/projects/[repo]/` vào folder đó
- Push/pull qua git như bình thường

input.content:
# Claude Session Sync

Folder này chứa Claude Code session history để sync giữa các máy qua GitHub.

## Cách dùng

```bash
# Lưu sessions từ máy hiện tại lên repo
./scripts/claude-save.sh

# Khôi phục sessions từ repo về máy hiện tại
./scripts/claude-restore.sh
```

## Workflow khi chuyển máy

**Trên máy cũ (trước khi rời):**
```bash
./scripts/claude-save.sh
git add .claude-sessions/
git commit -m "chore: sync claude sessions"
git push
```

**Trên máy mới (sau khi pull):**
```bash
git pull
./scripts/claude-restore.sh
```

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Sync Claude Code sessions từ local → repo

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g' | sed 's|^-||')"
DEST="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions"

if [ ! -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ]; then
  echo "❌ Không tìm thấy Claude session dir: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
  exit 1
fi

echo "📂 Source: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
echo "📦 Dest:   $DEST"

# Copy tất cả session files và memory
rsync -av --delete \
  --exclude=".gitkeep" \
  "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/" "$DEST/sessions/"

echo ""
echo "✅ Saved. Giờ commit và push:"
echo "   git add .claude-sessions/"
echo "   git commit -m \"chore: sync claude sessions\""
echo "   git push"

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Restore Claude Code sessions từ repo → local

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g' | sed 's|^-||')"
SRC="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions/sessions"

if [ ! -d "$SRC" ]; then
  echo "❌ Không có sessions trong repo. Chạy claude-save.sh trên máy kia trước."
  exit 1
fi

echo "📂 Source: $SRC"
echo "📦 Dest:   $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

# Cảnh báo nếu đã có sessions local
if [ -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ] && [ "$(ls -A "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "⚠️  Máy này đã có Claude sessions. Restore sẽ ghi đè."
  read -p "Tiếp tục? (y/N): " confirm
  [[ "$confirm" != "y" && "$confirm" != "Y" ]] && echo "Hủy." && exit 0
fi

mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

#!/bin/bash
# Restore Claude Code sessions từ repo → local

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g' | sed 's|^-||')"
SRC="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions/sessions"

if [ ! -d "$SRC" ]; then
  echo "❌ Không có sessions trong repo. Chạy claude-save.sh trên máy kia trước."
  exit 1
fi

echo "📂 Source: $SRC"
echo "📦 Dest:   $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

# Cảnh báo nếu đã có sessions local
if [ -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ] && [ "$(ls -A "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "⚠️  Máy này đã có Claude sessions. Restore sẽ ghi đè."
  read -p "Tiếp tục? (y/N): " confirm
  [[ "$confirm" != "y" && "$confirm" != "Y" ]] && echo "Hủy." && exit 0
fi

mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
rsync -av --delete "$SRC/" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/"

echo ""
echo "✅ Restored. Mở lại Claude Code để load sessions."

content.thinking:
The script is not finding the right path. The issue is in the path transformation. The actual path is:
`~/.claude/projects/-Users-nguyenduc-Personal-Repositories-tradex-monitoring/`

But the script generated:
`/Users/nguyenduc/.claude/projects/Users-nguyenduc-Personal-Repositories-tradex-monitoring`

The issue is that:
1. The leading `-` is being stripped by `sed 's|^-||'`
2. But actually the Claude project directory has a leading `-` before `Users`


3. Claude's naming convention replaces `/` with `-`, so the leading `/` becomes a leading `-`, and my sed command that removes the leading `-` is stripping the wrong character.

content.text: Path transformation sai — cần fix script:

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Sync Claude Code sessions từ local → repo

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g' | sed 's|^-||')"
DEST="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions"

if [ ! -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ]; then
  echo "❌ Không tìm thấy Claude session dir: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
  exit 1
fi

echo "📂 Source: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
echo "📦 Dest:   $DEST"

# Copy tất cả session files và memory
rsync -av --delete \
  --exclude=".gitkeep" \
  "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/" "$DEST/sessions/"

echo ""
echo "✅ Saved. Giờ commit và push:"
echo "   git add .claude-sessions/"
echo "   git commit -m \"chore: sync claude sessions\""
echo "   git push"

content.text:
Script chạy thành công. Tất cả 5 session files + memory folder đã được copy vào `.claude-sessions/sessions/`.

---

## Tóm tắt setup

**Files đã tạo:**
- [`.claude-sessions/`](.claude-sessions/) — chứa session data (sẽ commit lên GitHub)
- [`scripts/claude-save.sh`](scripts/claude-save.sh) — lưu sessions từ local → repo
- [`scripts/claude-restore.sh`](scripts/claude-restore.sh) — khôi phục sessions từ repo → local

**Workflow từ giờ:**

```bash
# Trên máy này — trước khi sang máy khác:
./scripts/claude-save.sh
git add .claude-sessions/
git commit -m "chore: sync claude sessions"
git push

# Trên máy khác — sau khi clone/pull repo:
git pull
./scripts/claude-restore.sh
```

**Lưu ý:** Script dùng `rsync --delete`, tức là luôn lấy máy chạy `save` làm "master". Nếu cả 2 máy đều có sessions mới, cần `save` từ máy muốn giữ trước. Bạn có muốn commit và push session hiện tại ngay không?

content: tôi muốn workflow này là tự động giữa các máy để tôi có thể làm việc xuyên suốt giữa các máy

message.content: tôi muốn workflow này là tự động giữa các máy để tôi có thể làm việc xuyên suốt giữa các máy

content.thinking:
The user wants automatic sync between machines, so they can work seamlessly across machines without manually running save/restore scripts. 

Let me think about how to achieve this:

1. **Git hooks approach**: 
   - `post-commit` hook: after each commit, also sync sessions
   - But this would mix session sync with regular commits, which is messy

2. **Claude Code hooks approach**:
   - Claude Code supports hooks in settings.json that run before/after certain events
   - We could add a `PreToolUse` or `PostToolUse` hook, or a stop hook
   - There's an `update-config` skill for configuring settings.json, and a "stop" hook would run when Claude Code exits - perfect for auto-saving

3. **Git hooks**:
   - `post-merge` hook: after `git pull`, auto-restore sessions
   - `pre-push` hook: before `git push`, auto-save sessions
   - This keeps session syncing tied to your actual code push/pull workflow

4. **Cron job approach**:
   - A periodic cron that auto-saves and pushes
   - But this creates too many commits

The best approach for "seamless across machines" would be using git hooks - `pre-push` to auto-save before pushing code, and `post-merge` to auto-restore after pulling.

**Option B: Claude Code hooks (stop hook)**
- When Claude Code session ends → auto save + commit + push
- When Claude Code starts → auto pull + restore
- This is more "Claude-aware" but requires modifying Claude Code settings

**Option C: Combine both**
- Claude Code stop hook → save + commit + push sessions
- Git post-merge hook → restore sessions

I'm leaning toward combining both approaches: the Claude Code stop hook handles saving and pushing when a session ends, while the git post-merge hook handles restoring sessions when pulling on another machine. This way sessions sync automatically without needing extra commands, though I need to think through whether auto-pushing on every session end creates too many commits.

Actually, using a dedicated `claude-sessions` branch might be cleaner—it keeps session data separate from the main branch history and avoids polluting commits. The workflow would be: save sessions to that branch on session end, then restore from it when starting on another machine. That feels more practical than trying to auto-commit to the main branch where it could conflict with uncommitted changes or fail silently if there's no internet. The `Stop` hook will trigger when the session ends, so I can use that to automatically save, commit, and push the session state. For the restore side, I'll add a `post-merge` git hook to pull in updated sessions after a git pull, and maybe a `post-checkout` hook to handle branch switches. The core idea is to keep sessions synced across machines by persisting them to git whenever Claude Code stops and reloading them when the repository updates.

Writing hook configuration... Writing shell scripts...

Actually, I think the Claude Code stop hook is the cleanest approach—it'll automatically save and push sessions whenever a session ends, which gives us the most seamless syncing without requiring the user to manually trigger anything. I should use the `update-config` skill to set this up in settings.json so the harness can execute the hook when the stop event fires. after `git pull`

3. **Git pre-push hook** → also runs save before push (backup)

For the Claude Code hook, I need to check where `.claude/settings.json` lives for this project, or fall back to the global `~/.claude/settings.json`. Let me see what settings files are available.

Actually, I should use the `update-config` skill since it's built for this. But first I need to figure out what the hook command should look like. The stop hook needs to be a shell command that's robust, only commits if there are actual changes, and pushes quietly.

The tricky part is that using absolute paths makes the hook machine-specific and breaks portability. Since Claude Code hooks run with the project root as the working directory, I can use relative paths like `./scripts/claude-save.sh` instead.

So my approach is to store the hook in `.claude/settings.json` at the repo root, using relative paths for portability.

Designing the solution... I'm settling on using a `.githooks/` directory that gets committed to the repo, so the post-merge hook can be shared across machines—users just need to run `git config core.hooksPath .githooks` once locally, or I can automate that in an install script.

content.text:
Để làm tự động hoàn toàn, tôi sẽ kết hợp:
- **Claude Code Stop hook** → auto-save + commit + push khi kết thúc session
- **Git post-merge hook** → auto-restore khi `git pull` trên máy khác
- **`.githooks/`** folder (committable) → hook sync được giữa các máy

Bắt đầu implement:

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-save Claude sessions + commit + push (chạy tự động khi session kết thúc)

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g')"
DEST="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions/sessions"

[ ! -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ] && exit 0

# Copy sessions vào repo
rsync -a --delete --exclude=".gitkeep" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/" "$DEST/" 2>/dev/null

# Commit nếu có thay đổi
cd "$REPO_DIR" || exit 0
git add .claude-sessions/ 2>/dev/null
git diff --staged --quiet && exit 0

git commit -m "chore: auto-sync claude sessions [skip ci]" --no-gpg-sign -q 2>/dev/null
git push --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-restore Claude sessions sau khi git pull/merge

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
SRC="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions/sessions"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g')"

[ ! -d "$SRC" ] && exit 0

# Chỉ restore nếu .claude-sessions có thay đổi trong merge này
if git diff-tree -r --name-only --no-commit-id ORIG_HEAD HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^\.claude-sessions/"; then
  mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

toolUseResult.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-restore Claude sessions sau khi git pull/merge

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
SRC="$REPO_DIR/.claude-sessions/sessions"
CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$(echo "$REPO_DIR" | sed 's|/|-|g')"

[ ! -d "$SRC" ] && exit 0

# Chỉ restore nếu .claude-sessions có thay đổi trong merge này
if git diff-tree -r --name-only --no-commit-id ORIG_HEAD HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^\.claude-sessions/"; then
  mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
  rsync -a --delete "$SRC/" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/" 2>/dev/null
  echo "✅ Claude sessions restored từ repo."
fi

git add .gitignore .claude/settings.json .githooks/ scripts/ .claude-sessions/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Add automatic Claude session sync between machines

- scripts/claude-autosave.sh: saves sessions + commits + pushes (triggered by Stop hook)
- scripts/claude-save.sh / claude-restore.sh: manual sync helpers
- scripts/install-hooks.sh: one-time setup per machine
- .githooks/post-merge: auto-restore sessions after git pull
- .claude/settings.json: Stop hook wires claude-autosave.sh on session end
- .claude-sessions/: stores session JSONL files and memory

Setup on new machine: run ./scripts/install-hooks.sh once after cloning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Đã push xong. Đây là toàn bộ workflow tự động:

---

## Workflow tự động (từ giờ)

**Máy hiện tại (đã setup):**
| Trigger | Hành động tự động |
|---------|------------------|
| Kết thúc Claude session | Save sessions → commit → push GitHub |
| `git pull` | Restore sessions từ repo về local |

**Trên máy mới (chạy 1 lần sau khi clone):**
```bash
git clone git@github.com:ducnmnhsv/tradex-monitoring.git
cd tradex-monitoring
./scripts/install-hooks.sh   # cài post-merge hook + restore sessions
```

Sau đó mọi thứ tự động — `git pull` sẽ tự restore sessions, và Claude Code sẽ tự save + push khi bạn đóng session.

> **Lưu ý:** `--no-gpg-sign` được dùng trong autosave để tránh lỗi nếu GPG không configured trên máy kia. Nếu bạn dùng GPG signing thì cần điều chỉnh [`scripts/claude-autosave.sh`](scripts/claude-autosave.sh).
